title: Lens prompt catalog and per-lens verdict telemetry
description: Add a built-in lens prompt catalog (correctness, security, regression, quality) that maps a lens name to a focused prompt fragment, with a graceful generic fallback for custom lens strings, and surface each lens's pass/fail verdict via the existing telemetry/history so a panel failure is attributable to a specific lens. Polish on top of sprint 1; the off-by-default guarantee and fan-out bounding remain intact.

successCriteria.description: A new module (e.g. src/orchestrator/eval-lenses.ts) exports a lens catalog resolving 'correctness','security','regression','quality' to DISTINCT non-empty focus prompt fragments, plus a resolver that returns a generic focus for any unknown custom lens string without throwing. A unit test asserts the four built-ins are distinct and that an unknown lens returns the generic fallback.

runAgentEvaluation uses the catalog to build each lens's focus block (replacing the inline string from sprint 1). With the catalog wired, the off path (panel disabled / <2 lenses) STILL makes exactly one judge call with no lens focus — proven by the sprint-1 off-path test staying green.

When the panel runs, each lens's verdict (lens name + passed) is emitted via the existing emit() telemetry and/or appendHistory, attributable by lens name. A unit test (telemetry/history spy or temp .bober) asserts one verdict record per lens with the correct lens name and pass/fail.
Fan-out bounding (peak concurrency <= maxConcurrent) and the off-by-default single-call guarantee are covered by tests that remain green after the catalog + telemetry changes.
npx tsc --noEmit, npm run build, and npx eslint src/ exit 0; the full vitest suite stays green with no new failures beyond the documented flaky tool-count baseline.

nonGoals: Do not change the reconcile semantics or the combine logic.
Do not add lens weighting or non-majority voting.
Do not emit telemetry on the off path beyond what the evaluator already emits today.
Do not touch the workflow engine, .claude/workflows, agents/*.md, or providers/types.ts.
stopConditions: Stop when the catalog resolves the four built-ins + generic fallback, per-lens verdicts are emitted, all guarantees hold, and typecheck/build/lint/tests are green.
Stop when changes are confined to src/orchestrator/eval-lenses.ts (+ its test), src/orchestrator/evaluator-agent.ts, and the evaluator-agent test.
definitionOfDone: A built-in lens catalog maps correctness/security/regression/quality to distinct focus fragments with a generic fallback for custom lenses; runAgentEvaluation uses it; each lens's verdict is emitted to telemetry/history attributable by name; the off-by-default single-call guarantee and maxConcurrent bounding still hold; typecheck/build/lint and the full suite are green.
assumptions: emit() (src/telemetry/emit.ts:69) is the telemetry entry point and is fire-and-forget; appendHistory is available for a durable per-lens record if telemetry is disabled.
The reconciler already returns lensVerdicts / the per-lens EvalResult[] is available in runAgentEvaluation before reconcile, so emitting per-lens verdicts needs no reducer change.
Custom (non-catalog) lens strings are valid and should produce a generic focus, not an error.
generatorNotes: Create src/orchestrator/eval-lenses.ts exporting a catalog, e.g. `const LENS_CATALOG: Record<string,string> = { correctness: '...focus on whether the implementation actually satisfies each success criterion...', security: '...focus on injection, auth, secret handling, unsafe input...', regression: '...focus on whether previously working behavior still works / pre-existing tests...', quality: '...focus on principles violations, dead code, naming, smells...' }` and `export function resolveLensFocus(lens: string): string` returning the catalog entry or a generic `Evaluate specifically through the '${lens}' lens.` fallback. Keep fragments distinct and non-empty. In src/orchestrator/evaluator-agent.ts: replace the sprint-1 inline lens string with resolveLensFocus(lens) in runSingleLensEval. After running the lenses and BEFORE/AFTER reconcile, iterate the per-lens EvalResult[] and emit each: `void emit(projectRoot, config, 'eval-lens-verdict', { contractId, lens, passed: r.passed })` (follow the existing emit call sites in evaluator-agent.ts / pipeline.ts for event-name + payload conventions; if 'eval-lens-verdict' must be a known event type, either reuse an existing generic event or add the minimal event-name plumbing — keep it consistent with how emit is already used). Only emit on the PANEL path (>=2 lenses), never on the off path. If emit's event enum is closed and adding a value is out of scope, fall back to appendHistory with an event the HistoryEntrySchema accepts (check src/state/history.ts event enum) — prefer the lowest-friction option that the schema allows without broad changes. Create src/orchestrator/eval-lenses.test.ts: assert the 4 built-ins are distinct + non-empty and an unknown lens returns the generic fallback. Extend evaluator-agent.test.ts: assert per-lens verdict emission (spy on emit or read temp history) with correct lens names + pass/fail, and that the off path still emits no per-lens verdicts and makes one call. Use import type, ESM .js specifiers, unicode section headers. Conventional commit: bober(sprint-2): add lens prompt catalog + per-lens verdict telemetry.
evaluatorNotes: Confirm eval-lenses.ts catalog: correctness/security/regression/quality map to distinct non-empty fragments; resolveLensFocus('made-up') returns the generic fallback (no throw). Confirm runAgentEvaluation uses resolveLensFocus and that the off path still makes exactly one call with NO lens focus (sprint-1 off-path test green). Verify per-lens verdict emission: spy on emit() (or inspect temp .bober history/telemetry) and assert one record per lens with the lens name + correct passed flag, and that NO per-lens verdicts are emitted on the off path. Confirm whichever emit/appendHistory path was used validates against the existing schema (no schema-breaking event). Run the fan-out bounding test (peak concurrency <= maxConcurrent) and the off-by-default single-call test — both green. Run npx tsc --noEmit, npm run build, npx eslint src/ (exit 0) and the full npx vitest run (tolerate only the flaky tool-count baseline). Confirm git diff confined to eval-lenses.ts(+test) and evaluator-agent.ts(+test); reconciler.ts, combine logic, and the event/history schemas unchanged (or minimally + validly extended).
